findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 28. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: The central shellstock tag diagram — a bordered example tag with annotation arrows connecting explanatory notes to specific tag fields — is completely absent from the HTML. This is a substantive diagram/illustration showing the required tag layout and field placement.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a bordered 'SALE TAG' box with directional arrows linking explanatory annotations (e.g., 'Harvest Date; may be mo/dy/yr…', 'Dealer Name, Address, & Certification Number') to their corresponding tag fields. The HTML renders all this content as flat linear text with no diagram, no box, and no arrows.

findings.description: The PDF uses a three-region spatial layout (left-side annotations, center tag, right-side annotations) where the spatial position conveys which annotation relates to which part of the tag. The HTML collapses this into a single linear column, losing the spatial relationships between annotations and tag fields.
findings.evidence: PDF: left-side annotations (e.g., 'Harvest Date; may be mo/dy/yr…', '90 Day tag retention notice') and right-side annotations (e.g., 'Dealer Name, Address, & Certification Number', 'Type of shellfish: oysters, clams, or mussels') are visually linked to specific tag regions. HTML: all annotations are interspersed linearly with the tag content, removing the visual grouping.

findings.description: The PDF contains a substantive visual diagram of an example shellstock sale tag (a bordered box layout with annotation arrows pointing from explanatory callouts to specific parts of the tag). This diagram/illustration is missing from the HTML — no tag is present. The text content of the tag and annotations is reproduced via a definition list, but the visual representation of the tag layout is lost.
findings.evidence:
PDF shows a bordered sale tag diagram with arrows connecting callout labels to tag fields. HTML has zero tags; content is rendered as a
definition list instead.

findings.description: The HTML adds two section headings that do not appear anywhere in the source PDF: 'Example Shellstock Tag with Explanations' and 'Tag Format and Handling Requirements'. These are fabricated headings not present in the original document.
findings.evidence:
PDF has only the title 'SHELLSTOCK TAG INFORMATION REQUIREMENTS' as a heading, with no sub-section headings. HTML adds

Example Shellstock Tag with Explanations

and

Tag Format and Handling Requirements

.
